predicate.title: Lead time to merge, agent cycle time from activity evidence, split dashboard charts
predicate.problem: the dashboard's lead and cycle bars are near-identical (both end at the merge commit, starts minutes apart) and mostly invisible (no outlier handling — one shared linear axis autoscaled off a 1020-minute outlier plus negative bootstrap bars); roadmap-priority-4-agent-cycle-time redefines lead time as merged_at minus opened_at and cycle time as the agent's sessionized active working time from agent-activity/v1 evidence, and CR-20260716-003 made that evidence available — metrics.py and the metric definitions must now consume it
acceptance_criteria: spec/tools/metrics.py computes lead_time_s as merged_at minus change.json opened_at (intent-based start dropped) and agent cycle time from evidence/activity.json, n/a with no backfill for rows predating the predicate

the dashboard replaces the shared-axis grouped chart with a lead chart whose axis is capped at the p90 (overflow bars run to the plot edge behind an axis-break chevron with the exact value labeled) and a separate agent-cycle chart with its own axis; negative bootstrap bars stay plotted

metric-lead-time and metric-cycle-time are redefined in place with History paragraphs recording the v1 definitions; roadmap-priority-4-agent-cycle-time flips to done and roadmap-whats-next promotes roadmap-slice-2-authority-and-fail-safe in this same CR per the maintenance rule
spec/tools/check.py passes and all three metrics.py output modes (markdown, --json, --html) render the new columns
